story: China's modern higher education began in 1895 with the Imperial Tientsin University which was a polytechnic plus a law department. Liberal arts were not offered until three years later at Capital University. To this day, about half of China's elite universities remain essentially polytechnical.
q1: The Imperial Tientsin University, which launched China's modern higher education in 1895, was also the first Chinese institution to offer liberal arts courses. (True/False?)
